2604.05779.title: What Models Know, How Well They Know It: Knowledge-Weighted Fine-Tuning for Learning When to Say "I Don't Know"
2604.05779.paper_summary: While large language models (LLMs) demonstrate strong capabilities across diverse user queries, they still suffer from hallucinations, often arising from knowledge misalignment between pre-training and fine-tuning. To address this misalignment, we reliably estimate a fine-grained, instance-level knowledge score via multi-sampled inference. Using the knowledge score, we scale the learning signal according to the model's existing knowledge, while encouraging explicit "I don't know" responses for out-of-scope queries. Experimental results show that this approach allows the model to explicitly express uncertainty when it lacks knowledge, while maintaining accuracy on questions it can answer. Furthermore, we propose evaluation metrics for uncertainty, showing that accurate discrimination between known and unknown instances consistently improves performance.
2604.05779.paper_summary_zh: 雖然大型語言模型（LLMs）在多樣化的用戶查詢中展現出強大的能力，但它們仍然會遭遇幻覺，這通常源於預訓練與微調之間的知識不一致。為了解決這種不一致，我們通過多次抽樣推斷可靠地估計出細粒度的實例級知識分數。利用知識分數，我們根據模型現有的知識來調整學習信號，同時鼓勵對於超出範疇的查詢給予明確的「我不知道」回應。實驗結果顯示，這種方法使模型在缺乏知識時能夠明確表達不確定性，同時在能回答的問題上保持準確性。此外，我們提出了不確定性的評估指標，顯示出對已知和未知實例的準確區分持續改善了性能。
2604.05779.author: Joosung Lee et.al.
2604.05779.authors: Joosung Lee, Hwiyeol Jo, Donghyeon Ko, Kyubyung Chae, Cheonbok Park, Jeonghoon Kim
